docs.text:
Technology Professional with 12+ Years of Experience E-commerce Domain Expert AI & Machine Learning Practitioner

Turning years of industry experience into practical AI solutions through continuous learning, hands-on projects, and public knowledge sharing.

## 👋 About Me **Roja Rani Repalle** - 💼 12+ Years Industry Experience - 🛒 E-commerce Domain Expertise - 🤖 AI & Machine Learning Practitioner - 📚 Building in Public - 🚀 Future AI Engineer & Solution Architect Combining business expertise, technology experience, and modern AI capabilities to build intelligent, scalable, and business-driven solutions. ## 🛣️ Learning Roadmap ### ✅ Completed - Python - NumPy - Pandas ### 🚧 In Progress - Machine Learning - Analytics Engine ### ⏳ Upcoming - Deep Learning - Computer Vision - NLP - Generative AI - Agentic AI ## 📊 Progress Tracker | Topic | Progress | |--------|----------| | Python | 100% | | NumPy | 100% | | Pandas | 100% | | Machine Learning | 30% | | Deep Learning | 0% | | Computer Vision | 0% | | NLP | 0% | | Generative AI | 10% | | Agentic AI | 5% |

docs.title: 1. Numbers, Arithmetic Operators
docs.text:
1. Numbers, Arithmetic Operators Numbers In [1]: 
3
Out[1]: 
3
In [2]: 
5
Out[2]: 
5
Arithmetic Operators Operation Operator Description Addition + Adds two values Subtraction - Subtracts the right operand from the left Multiplication * Multiplies two values Division / Divides operands and returns a float (e.g., 5 / 2 = 2.5) Floor Division // Divides operands and returns an integer, rounding down (e.g., 5 // 2 = 2) Modulus % Returns the remainder of a division (e.g., 5 % 2 = 1) Exponentiation ** Raises one number to the power of another In [18]: 
5 + 3     # Addition
Out[18]: 
8
In [19]: 
8 - 3     # Subtraction
Out[19]: 
5
In [20]: 
5 * 4     # Multiplication
Out[20]: 
20
In [21]: 
8 / 2     # Division
Out[21]: 
4.0
In [22]: 
9 // 2    # Floor Division
Out[22]: 
4
In [23]: 
6 % 2     # Modulus
Out[23]: 
0
In [24]: 
3 ** 2    # Exponentiation
Out[24]: 
9
Role of Underscore(_) Python automatically stores the value of the last expression in the interpreter to a particular variable called "_." We can also assign these value to another variable if you want. In [14]: 
5 + 4
Out[14]: 
9
In [15]: 
_         # stores the result of the above expression
Out[15]: 
9
In [16]: 
_ + 6
Out[16]: 
15
In [26]: 
a = _     # assigning the value of _ to another variable
In [8]: 
a * 3
Out[8]: 
21
BODMAS (or PEMDAS) BODMAS Rule Python Operator Description Brackets () Highest priority; forces order Orders ** Exponents/Powers Division /, //, % Standard, floor division, and remainder Multiplication * Multiplication (equal priority with Division) Addition + Addition Subtraction - Subtraction In [27]: 
4 + 5 - 3 * 10      # (mas - mul,add,sub)
Out[27]: 
-21
In [28]: 
4 + ( 5 - 10 ) * 3  # BODMAS
Out[28]: 
-11
In [29]: 
4 / ( 5 - 10 ) * 3  # BODMAS
Out[29]: 
-2.4000000000000004
Example Calculation

Expression: 10 + 5 * (2 ** 3) - 6 / 2

Step 1 (Brackets): (2 ** 3) → 8 Step 2 (Multiplication): 5 * 8 → 40 Step 3 (Division): 6 / 2 → 3.0 Step 4 (Addition/Subtraction): 10 + 40 - 3.0 → 50 - 3.0 → 47.0

Final Answer: 47.0

In [30]: 
10 + 5 * (2 ** 3) - 6 / 2
Out[30]: 
47.0
